channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Chalobah replaces injured Livramento for World Cup
item.description: Newcastle United full-back Tino Livramento is out of England's World Cup campaign with a calf injury and is replaced by Chelsea defender Trevoh Chalobah.

item.title: Tuchel's England deal includes performance clause
item.description: Thomas Tuchel's England contract extension is subject to a performance clause, says FA chief executive Mark Bullingham.

item.title: Tunisia sack Lamouchi just one game into World Cup
item.description: Tunisia sack Sabri Lamouchi one game into their World Cup, with former Morocco and Saudi Arabia manager Herve Renard replacing him.

item.title: Lack of World Cup segregation a risk - fans' chief
item.description: The head of fan group Football Supporters Europe says a lack of fan segregation at the 2026 World Cup shows Fifa has "lost control of their ticketing".

item.title: Record draws and Europe's slow start - is the World Cup lacking jeopardy?
item.description: Seven of the 10 European teams to have played at the World Cup so far have failed to win - is heat the issue?

item.title: Stag-do drag to World Cup - the rise of Scotland's other national anthem
item.description: From stag-do drag to Fenway Park, how did a 1970s disco hit become the soundtrack of the Tartan Army?

item.title: Meet the Iraq player set to make history for Pakistan
item.description: Zidane Iqbal, a former Manchester United player, will make history when he plays for Iraq this summer, becoming the first player of Pakistani heritage to feature at a men's World Cup.

item.title: Solskjaer in the running for Ipswich job
item.description: Ole Gunnar Solskjaer and Gary O'Neil are among the leading contenders for the Ipswich managerial role.

item.title: Just's rise from Austrian second tier to World Cup breakout star
item.description: BBC Scotland charts a rapid rise that has taken the Motherwell attacking midfielder from second-division football to the game's grandest stage.

item.title: What does future hold for Rashford as deadline passes?
item.description: Barcelona decided not to trigger the option that would have allowed them to sign Marcus Rashford for £26m, so what does the future hold for the England World Cup star?

item.title: Just scores double but New Zealand held by Iran in entertaining game
item.description: Iran twice come from behind to draw 2-2 with New Zealand at the Los Angeles Stadium in their Group G encounter.

item.title: Jordan's journey from Harry Redknapp to date with Messi
item.description: When they face world champions Argentina in their final group stage match on 27 June, it may be Jordan's own World Cup final.

item.title: Would Hearts' McInnes be 'perfect fit' for Rangers?
item.description: With Derek McInnes reportedly on the verge of a switch from Hearts to Rangers, his former assistant Tony Docherty details why the move would be a 'perfect fit'.

item.title: 'Not a model' - Bielsa refuses to engage with World Cup photoshoot
item.description: Uruguay and former Leeds coach Marcelo Bielsa defends his stony-faced appearance in the official World Cup photoshoot.

item.title: Dazzling winger to strolling veteran - Messi back on World Cup stage
item.description: Lionel Messi is set for his sixth World Cup with Argentina. Guillem Balague looks at how the Argentine has evolved.

item.title: Why Kante is still crucial for France - Giroud
item.description: France's World Cup winner Olivier Giroud explains why N'Golo Kante is still an important part of Didier Deschamps' squad, eight years after planning to retire from international football.

item.title: Ghana challenges Partey's visa denial in court
item.description: Ghana's government files for a review of Canada's refusal to grant midfielder Thomas Partey a visa, with the case due to be heard by a Canadian court.

item.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: Saudi Arabia hold off relentless Uruguay to earn draw
item.description: Saudi Arabia hold firm against relentless pressure to earn a 1-1 draw against two-time world champions Uruguay in their Group H opener at the Miami Stadium.

item.title: Lukaku makes impact off the bench as Belgium draw with Egypt
item.description: An own goal from Mohamed Hany denies Egypt a first ever victory at the World Cup as they draw 1-1 with Belgium at the Seattle Stadium.

item.title: Vozinha heroics help Cape Verde hold Spain to goalless draw
item.description: European champions Spain were held to surprise 0-0 draw by debutants Cape Verde in their World Cup opener, with 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha producing a standout performance for his side in their Group H clash.

item.title: 'Huge goal!' - Araujo equalises for Uruguay against Saudi Arabia
item.description: Maxi Araujo equalises in the 80th minute for Uruguay against Saudi Arabia at the Miami Stadium in their Group H encounter.

item.title: 'Sensational!' - Wood and Just combine twice for New Zealand goals
item.description: Chris Wood and Elijah Just combine for two 'sensational' goals for New Zealand during their Group G match with Iran at the Los Angeles Stadium.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 9
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: What's next for Curtis Jones as Inter Milan step up their interest?
item.description: As Inter Milan step up their interest in Curtis Jones, a look at the decision facing Liverpool and Jones, whose contract expires next summer

item.title: VAR official says hand gesture was 'involuntary, subconscious twitch'
item.description: Video assistant referee Shaun Evans has denied "intentionally" making a hand gesture "to communicate a message, affiliation, game or belief of any kind" and claimed it was a "an involuntary, subconscious twitch" that he was "unaware" of.

item.title: Are billions flooding into Saudi Pro League helping national team?
item.description: As Saudi Arabia earn creditable point against Uruguay, is the money flooding into the Saudi Pro League helping the national team?

item.title: Iran v Iran in the stands as politics and football intertwine
item.description: The Iranian team may want to unite people but what the game with New Zealand revealed is just how divided many Iranians remain, writes BBC North America correspondent Shaimaa Khalil.

item.title: New Leicester boss Martin 'must show he can adapt'
item.description: New Leicester City boss Russell Martin must show he is adaptable after recent failings, says ex-Foxes defender Matt Heath.

item.title: Iran 'most oppressed' team at World Cup - Ghalenoei
item.description: Head coach Amir Ghalenoei says his Iran side are the "most oppressed in the World Cup" after having last-minute travel restrictions imposed on them.

item.title: 'My word!' - Ashour's long-range strikes put Egypt ahead
item.description: Egypt's Emam Ashour scores the opener in their Group G fixture against Belgium in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Lukaku's instant impact for Belgium's equaliser
item.description: Romelu Lukaku has an instant impact for Belgium as he plays a key part in their equalising goal against Egypt in Seattle, just 22 seconds after coming off the bench.

item.title: 'He's the story' - Vozinha's epic goalkeeping display thwarts Spain
item.description: Watch Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha produce a masterclass in goalkeeping to help his team record a 0-0 draw again Spain in their Group H fixture at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Emotional full-time scenes as Cape Verde celebrate 0-0 draw against Spain
item.description: Watch the emotional full-time scenes following Cape Verde's 0-0 draw against Spain in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Gyokeres & Isak score as Sweden put five past Tunisia
item.description: Watch highlights as Sweden get their World Cup campaign off to an emphatic start, scoring five goals in a win over Group F opponents Tunisia.

item.title: Amad scores late winner as Ivory Coast beat Ecuador
item.description: Manchester United winger Amad Diallo scored a superb 90th-minute winner as Ivory Coast beat Ecuador in Philadelphia.

item.title: Japan come from behind twice to draw with Netherlands
item.description: Daichi Kamada's late header rescues a deserved point for Japan against the Netherlands in their opening World Cup game in Dallas.

item.title: 'Thunderbolt!' - Sweden's Ayari scores stunner but refuses to celebrate
item.description: Yasin Ayari scores a stunner for Sweden against Tunisia but refuses to celebrate. The Brighton midfielder could have represented the opposition as his father is Tunisian.

item.title: Shamrock Rovers fans 'immensely proud' of Cape Verde hero Lopes
item.description: Shamrock Rovers defender Roberto 'Pico' Lopes says his side's draw with highly-fancied Spain in their first-ever World Cup match felt "a bit surreal" post-match.

item.title: I'll keep gambling with fitness for England - Saka
item.description: Bukayo Saka says he will continue gambling with his fitness and is "ready to go" for England's World Cup opener against Croatia on Wednesday.

item.title: Scotland captain Weir joins Champions League runners-up Lyon
item.description: Scotland captain Caroline Weir signs a three-year contract with French champions Lyon after leaving Real Madrid.

item.title: Football Daily World Cup quiz: England v Croatia flashback
item.description: How many of England's starting line-up can you name from the last time they played Croatia at the World Cup?

item.title: Real make Fernandes contact - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Real Madrid make Mateus Fernandes contact, Manchester United not willing to meet West Ham's asking price and Bayern Munich to sign Ismael Saibari.

item.title: The 40-year-old keeper who inspired Cape Verde's historic debut against Spain
item.description: Cape Verde stuns Spain with a goalless draw inspired by a 40-year-old goalkeeper who has gone viral on social media.

item.title: 'Cartwheels at breakfast' & kicking about Boston - the culture shift in Scotland camp
item.description: Steve Clarke "doing cartwheels" is a sign of the shift among the Scotland squad and staff, writes Amy Canavan in Charlotte.

item.title: False nines? 4-4-2? The tactical trends defining World Cup so far
item.description: The return of 4-4-2? False nines? BBC Sport looks at the tactical trends emerging at the World Cup so far.

item.title: 'Greatest feeling ever': Cape Verdeans tell BBC of joy at holding Spain to draw
item.description: The streets of the capital, Praia, shook with wild celebrations as the small island nation held Spain to a shock 0-0 draw.

item.title: Palace appoint Sage as head coach on three-year deal
item.description: Crystal Palace appoint Lens manager Pierre Sage as their head coach on a three-year contract to succeed Oliver Glasner.

item.title: World Cup hydration breaks - who are the winners and losers?
item.description: They have become a regular feature at World Cup games - but who are the winners and who are losers of the mandatory hydration breaks?

item.title: From last-chance saloon to World Cup redemption for Potter
item.description: For a manager who was dismissed by West Ham in September after failing at Chelsea before that, few would have expected Graham Potter to be steering a nation to a winning start in the World Cup this summer.

item.title: Why Haiti v Scotland was antidote to the ills of world football
item.description: It might have been nervy. It might not have been pretty. But Scotland are here. And Scotland have won. Tom English reports from Boston.

item.title: Who will win the World Cup? BBC pundits make their predictions
item.description: Who will win, who might spring a surprise and how will England and Scotland do? BBC pundits predict what will happen at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Any early shocks? Sutton's World Cup 2026 score predictions - opening group games
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the first round of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: The wild hip-hop parties that started Tuchel's journey to England boss
item.description: Thomas Tuchel is bidding to become the first foreign manager to win the World Cup. BBC Sport charts the start of his journey to becoming England boss.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?
item.description: The opening match of the 2026 World Cup was dominated by players wearing pink boots - but why is the colour so prevalent in football at the moment?

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Ask pundits McCann & Crichton your World Cup questions
item.description: BBC Sport Scotland pundits Neil McCann and Leanne Crichton are in Boston and are ready to answer - some - of your questions about Scotland's World Cup campaign.

item.title: 'McInnes and Rohl moves are done deals' - gossip
item.description: Barney Stewart is reportedly poised to be sold to West Bromwich Albion by Falkirk as head coach Danny Rohl's exit from Rangers to be replaced by Heart of Midlothian's Derek McInnes appears to move closer.

item.title: Six Lionesses made MBEs in King's Birthday Honours
item.description: Kevin Sinfield has received a knighthood in the King's Birthday Honours while six of England's Euro 2025-winning squad have been made MBEs.

item.title: Free agent Wales players put themselves in shop window
item.description: Rhian Wilkinson says her free-agent internationals gave potential suitors a glimpse of their qualities by helping Wales finish top of their Women's World Cup qualifying group.

item.title: Man City sign England's Mead on free transfer
item.description: Manchester City have confirmed the signing of England forward Beth Mead on a free transfer from Arsenal.

item.title: Weir's title regret as she confirms Real Madrid exit
item.description: Scotland midfielder Caroline Weir has confirmed her exit from Real Madrid after four years with the La Liga runners-up without giving a hint of her next destination.

item.title: Man City agree deal to sign England's Charles
item.description: Manchester City have agreed a deal to sign England defender Niamh Charles from Chelsea.

item.title: London Stadium and West Ham women's team not aware of Sullivan contact ban
item.description: The owner of West Ham United's home has raised concerns it was not told about safeguarding restrictions.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
